q: What are the three main interaction types in embedded systems?
A button and a single LED represent which kind of binary interface?
In a 4x4 keypad, how are rows and columns organized?
opts: Only rows are used for reading keys

q: What type of touch sensor is built with two conductive layers separated by spacers?
Which interaction method uses a head-mounted display to immerse the user in a virtual environment?
Which technology overlays digital information onto the real world?
The Wii controller is an example of which type of interaction?
What does the EEG-based human-machine interface measure?
Which library function redirects stdin/stdout to custom hardware in Arduino?
In the STDIO button/LED example, what character is returned by GetButton when pressed?
What is the main advantage of using STDIO with custom hardware streams?
opts: Same printf/scanf can be used for different I/O devices

q: An operating system in embedded context manages which resources?
The simplest software structure for an embedded application is:
What is a major drawback of the 'spin-lock' method for multi-tasking?
In timer-interrupt-based multi-tasking, tasks are executed:
opts: Only when an external button is pressed
Periodically, based on a recurrence time and offset
As fast as possible in a loop

q: A task has REC = 3 ms and OFST = 3 ms. What does this mean?
opts: Runs every 3 ms, first at 3 ms after start

q: In the timer ISR example, the task executes when the recurrence counter becomes:
opts: Less than or equal to zero

q: What is a sensor in embedded systems?
opts: A component that converts electrical energy to motion
Totality of components that transform a physical quantity into an internal signal
A device that only measures temperature

q: The component that converts a measurable physical value into an electrical signal is:
A sensor that provides only ON/OFF states belongs to which interface classification?
A sensor output that varies continuously in voltage (e.g. 0–5V) is classified as:
An ultrasonic distance sensor outputting a pulse width proportional to distance uses which interface?
A sensor that measures a parameter inside the system (e.g. CPU temperature) is called:
A GPS sensor that provides global location is an example of:
What does an amplifier do in hardware signal conditioning?
opts: Cuts the signal at a limit

q: In hardware conditioning, 'saturate' means:
opts: Cut the signal at a maximum or minimum level

q: A low-pass filter is used to:
opts: Smooth the signal by attenuating high frequencies

q: The Arduino constrain(x, min, max) function performs:
A median filter is effective against which type of noise?
A weighted average filter is a type of:
The formula (x−x1)/(x2−x1) = (y−y1)/(y2−y1) represents:
A virtual sensor:
opts: Is a physical sensor without wires
Computes a measurement from other sensor signals using a model

q: In the 3-DOF robotic arm example, the end effector position is derived from:
opts: Joint angles (β, γ, δ) and link lengths

q: An actuator in an embedded system:
opts: Converts internal electrical signal into a physical action

q: The interface of a simple DC motor controlled only ON/OFF is:
A servo motor receiving a PWM signal with varying pulse width uses which interface?
A stepper motor typically uses which type of electrical connection?
In a unipolar stepper motor, the current in each coil:
Which power conversion device uses an electromagnet to mechanically switch contacts?
In a transistor used as a switch, power dissipation is minimized because:
opts: The transistor is always in active region
Either voltage is nearly zero or current is nearly zero
It operates only at high frequency

q: An H-bridge allows:
opts: Reversing the direction of current through a motor
Only ON/OFF control of a motor

q: For a BLDC motor, the power stage typically uses:
Diagnosis in embedded systems is:
opts: The process of fixing a bug in code
Identification of the nature and cause of a phenomenon

q: Threshold symptom detection compares:
opts: The signal with two other signals
The signal value with a preset value (over/under)
The signal over a long time

q: Range symptom detection checks if a signal is:
Plausibility diagnosis relies on:
opts: Comparing the same parameter from two or more sensors/methods

q: 'Stall in range' diagnosis detects:
opts: No variation in the signal over time (signal stuck)

q: A binary (anti-bouncing) filter is used for:
opts: Qualifying a diagnosis by requiring the symptom to persist for a certain time

q: Which reaction to a qualified diagnosis means ignoring the symptom for a while?
Derating as a reaction means:
opts: Limiting functionality (e.g. reducing power) during the diagnosis

q: Protection by parameter trajectory control considers not only position but also:
In open-loop control, the output is determined:
opts: Based on a predefined relationship without measuring the controlled variable

q: On-Off control with hysteresis adds:
opts: A dead band to prevent rapid switching around the setpoint

q: Which term in a PID controller is proportional to accumulated error over time?
After setting Kp to half the oscillation value, what do you increase next in PID tuning?
Evolution (position) control typically uses:
opts: A predefined profile (trajectory) of speed/acceleration to reach a position

q: In communication theory, the 'channel' is:
opts: The medium through which the message travels

q: In serial communication, data is sent:
opts: All bits simultaneously on multiple wires
One bit at a time over a single line

q: SPI is a ________ protocol.
In SPI, the slave select (SS) pin:
opts: Selects which slave device communicates with the master

q: I2C uses how many wires (excluding power/ground)?
In a UART frame, the start bit is always:
What is the idle state of a UART line?
A program interpreter:
opts: Compiles code to machine language before execution
Reads and executes source code line-by-line

q: MicroPython on a microcontroller is an example of:
G-code is commonly used to control:
Morse code interpreter converts:
opts: Short and long pulses (dots/dashes) into characters

q: Fuzzy logic differs from classical logic by using:

In the knowledge base, a fuzzy rule typically has the form:
The final step converting a fuzzy output set back to a crisp control value is:
Which is a typical application of fuzzy control?
A Finite State Machine (FSM) consists of:
opts: A finite number of states, transitions, and actions

q: In a Moore FSM, the outputs depend on:
opts: Both the current state and the inputs

q: In a Mealy FSM, the outputs are a function of:
opts: The current state and the inputs

q: In Moore FSM evaluation, which step is performed first in the loop?
opts: Output evaluation based on current state

q: In the traffic light FSM example, state 'goN' means:
The traffic light FSM table uses input values 0 to 3 to represent:
In the line follower FSM, the output is a 2-bit value controlling:
The line follower FSM has three states. Which drives both motors forward?
The 'Layered Architecture' in sensor/actuator systems is used to:
opts: Separate hardware abstraction, signal processing, and application logic
Combine all code in one function

q: In the signal conditioning flow, 'virtual sensor' belongs to which layer?
Which is an example of an actuator for pressure control?
The 'Ideal Mechanical Advantage' (IMA) concept relates to:
opts: The mechanical transformation in a sensor mechanism

q: A relay provides what key property between control and power circuits?
In the transistor analogy, the base current controls:
Main heat generation in a linear-mode transistor occurs because:
opts: There is simultaneous voltage drop and current flow

q: In power conversion, commutation (switching) avoids high dissipation because:
opts: The transistor is always in the active region
The transistor is either fully off (I=0) or fully on (V≈0)

q: A bipolar stepper motor driver requires an H-bridge that can:
opts: Reverse current direction in each coil

q: Diagnosis qualification uses a binary filter (anti-bouncing) to:
opts: Ensure a symptom persists for a minimum time before accepted

q: 'Out of range protection' checks:
opts: The value before applying it, clamping if needed

q: In PID tuning, the first step after setting desired value is:
opts: Set Kp/Ki/Kd to 0, increase Kp until oscillation and halve it
Use trial and error with all three

q: The sampling time Δt in PID discrete equations appears in:
In 2WD platform control, differential steering is used to:
opts: Control robot orientation by varying left and right wheel speeds

q: The communication environment 'transmission media' includes:

opts: The structure of the message and rules for exchange

q: In the USART protocol, parity is used for:

The 'Gamer' experiment and TEDx mind interaction refer to:
In embedded systems, a 'signal' is defined as:
opts: The evolution of information in time
